perturbation.original_text: Recipient's obligations under this agreement sha ll not apply to any information which Recipient can document beyond any reasonable doubt was available to the public or known to or developed by the Recipient prior to the date first above written. Th ese obligations shall cease as to any information which Recipient can document thereafter became or becomes available to the public, otherwise than as a result of disclosure by Recipient or was or is disclosed to Recipient by a party unrelated to Recipient, that does not have a nondisclosure oblig ation with respect to it. Moreover, Recipient's obligations under this agreement shall not apply to Confidential In formation Recipient has a legal obligation to disclose to public authorities under la w, regulation or lawful administrative decision.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient's obligations under this agreement shall only not apply to any information which Recipient can document beyond any reasonable doubt was available to the public or known to or developed by the Recipient prior to the date first above written. These obligations shall not cease as to any information which Recipient can document thereafter became or becomes available to the public, regardless of disclosure by Recipient or disclosure to Recipient by a party unrelated to Recipient. Moreover, Recipient's obligations under this agreement shall still apply to Confidential In formation Recipient has a legal obligation to disclose to public authorities under law, regulation or lawful administrative decision.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states circumstances where Recipient's obligations do not apply. The modified text negates these exceptions. For example, previously, if information became public, the obligations ceased, but now they continue regardless. This contradicts the conditions for exceptions listed in the original agreement in section 3.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient's obligations under this agreement shall only not apply to any information which Recipient can document beyond any reasonable doubt was available to the public or known to or developed by the Recipient prior to the date first above written. These obligations shall not cease as to any information which Recipient can document thereafter became or becomes available to the public, regardless of disclosure by Recipient or disclosure to Recipient by a party unrelated to Recipient. Moreover, Recipient's obligations under this agreement shall still apply to Confidential In formation Recipient has a legal obligation to disclose to public authorities under law, regulation or lawful administrative decision.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the original text by negating the exceptions to the Recipient's obligations. Previously, certain circumstances, like information becoming public, would release the Recipient from their obligations. The changed text explicitly states that these obligations do not cease, regardless of these circumstances. This directly contradicts the original conditions under which the Recipient's obligations would not apply, creating a strong in-text contradiction as defined by structural flaws causing in-text contradictions.

perturbation.original_text: At any time, upon the respective Disclosing Party's request, Recipient shall promptly deliver to the respective Disclosing Party the Confidential Informa tion and all materials relating to or involving Confidential Information (whether prepared by th e respective Disclosing Party or otherwise), and will not retain any copies, extracts or other repr oductions in whole or in part regarding the Confidential Information. The delivery of such mate rial shall not relieve Recipient of its obligation of confidentiality or other obligations hereunder.
perturbation.changed_text: At any time, upon the respective Disclosing Party's request, Recipient shall promptly deliver to the respective Disclosing Party the Confidential Informa tion and all materials relating to or involving Confidential Information (whether prepared by th e respective Disclosing Party or otherwise), but Recipient is allowed to retain copies, extracts or other repr oductions in whole or in part regarding the Confidential Information for archival purposes. The delivery of such mate rial shall not relieve Recipient of its obligation of confidentiality or other obligations hereunder.
perturbation.explanation: The original text forbids the Recipient from keeping any copies of the confidential information, but the changed text allows it for archival purposes. This clearly contradicts the 'will not retain any copies' clause. The contradicting clause can be found on section 4.
perturbation.contradicted_text: At any time, upon the respective Disclosing Party's request, Recipient shall promptly deliver to the respective Disclosing Party the Confidential Informa tion and all materials relating to or involving Confidential Information (whether prepared by th e respective Disclosing Party or otherwise), but Recipient is allowed to retain copies, extracts or other repr oductions in whole or in part regarding the Confidential Information for archival purposes. The delivery of such mate rial shall not relieve Recipient of its obligation of confidentiality or other obligations hereunder.
perturbation.justification: The original text states the recipient 'will not retain any copies', but the changed text allows the recipient to retain copies for archival purposes. This creates a direct contradiction with the original clause, therefore a strong contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: Nothing in this agreement sh all be construed as granting Recipient any rights of any kinds in the Confidential Information, by license or otherwise.
perturbation.changed_text: Nothing in this agreement shall be construed as granting Recipient any rights of any kinds in the Confidential Information, by license or otherwise, except for the right to use the information internally for research purposes, provided such use does not directly harm the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.explanation: This modifies the original text to grant a specific right (internal research use) that was explicitly denied previously. This directly contradicts the statement that no rights are granted. This contradicts section 5, because the original says there should be no rights what so ever.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Nothing in this agreement shall be construed as granting Recipient any rights of any kinds in the Confidential Information, by license or otherwise, except for the right to use the information internally for research purposes, provided such use does not directly harm the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the recipient is not granted any rights, but the changed text grants the recipient a specific right (internal research use). This directly contradicts the initial statement, creating an in-text contradiction. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, but the explanation states this change in the text contradicts the original meaning.

perturbation.original_text: This agreement shall be governed by the laws of Belgium. All disputes or differences arising in connection with this agreement which cannot be settled amicably shall be finally settled by arbitration in Brussels under the Rules of Arbitra tion of the International Chamber of Commerce by one or more arbitrators to be appointed under the te rms of those rules. In any arbitration in which there are three arbitrators, the chairman shall be of juridical education. It shall be conducted in English. The award of the arbitration will be final and binding upon the parties concerned. The parties concerned may instead elect to resolve by mediation a dispute or difference arising in connection with this agreement which cannot be settled amicably.
perturbation.changed_text: This agreement shall be governed by the laws of Belgium, except for disputes involving intellectual property, which shall be governed by the laws of Germany. All disputes or differences arising in connection with this agreement which cannot be settled amicably shall be finally settled by arbitration in Brussels under the Rules of Arbitra tion of the International Chamber of Commerce by one or more arbitrators to be appointed under the te rms of those rules, unless the dispute concerns intellectual property, in which case it shall be litigated in the courts of Karlsruhe, Germany. In any arbitration in which there are three arbitrators, the chairman shall be of juridical education. It shall be conducted in English. The award of the arbitration will be final and binding upon the parties concerned. The parties concerned may instead elect to resolve by mediation a dispute or difference arising in connection with this agreement which cannot be settled amicably.
perturbation.explanation: The original text stipulates that all disputes are governed by Belgian law and resolved through arbitration in Brussels. The modified version introduces an exception: intellectual property disputes are governed by German law and litigated in German courts, contradicting the original universal agreement on governing law and dispute resolution. This directly contradicts section 6 of the agreement. There are contradicting dispute resolutions depending on the case type.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This agreement shall be governed by the laws of Belgium, except for disputes involving intellectual property, which shall be governed by the laws of Germany. All disputes or differences arising in connection with this agreement which cannot be settled amicably shall be finally settled by arbitration in Brussels under the Rules of Arbitra tion of the International Chamber of Commerce by one or more arbitrators to be appointed under the te rms of those rules, unless the dispute concerns intellectual property, in which case it shall be litigated in the courts of Karlsruhe, Germany. In any arbitration in which there are three arbitrators, the chairman shall be of juridical education. It shall be conducted in English. The award of the arbitration will be final and binding upon the parties concerned. The parties concerned may instead elect to resolve by mediation a dispute or difference arising in connection with this agreement which cannot be settled amicably.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an exception for intellectual property disputes, stating they are governed by German law and litigated in German courts, while the original text stipulated that ALL disputes are governed by Belgian law and resolved through arbitration in Brussels. Since the contradicted text is the same as the changed text, it contradicts itself by presenting two different legal frameworks for dispute resolution depending on the subject matter. Therefore, there is a clear and direct conflict within the document, which is a strong contradiction. This aligns with the definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This agreement shall come into force after signatu re of both parties hereto on the Effective Date and shall expire on February 28, 2009. The obligations pursuant to Article 2 shall remain valid for the Recipient even after the end of this agreemen t for a period of five (5) years thereafter.
perturbation.changed_text: This agreement shall come into force after signatu re of both parties hereto on the Effective Date and shall expire on February 28, 2009, unless Forschungszentrum determines that the Recipient's continued confidentiality is essential, in which case the agreement shall be extended indefinitely. The obligations pursuant to Article 2 shall remain valid for the Recipient even after the end of this agreemen t for a period of one (1) years thereafter.
perturbation.explanation: The original agreement has a defined expiration date, plus a fixed five-year period of confidentiality obligation after that date. The modified text adds a clause allowing Forschungszentrum to extend the agreement indefinitely, contradicting the original expiration date, as well as change the period from five to one. This introduces uncertainty and contradicts section 8, as the date can be variable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This agreement shall come into force after signatu re of both parties hereto on the Effective Date and shall expire on February 28, 2009, unless Forschungszentrum determines that the Recipient's continued confidentiality is essential, in which case the agreement shall be extended indefinitely. The obligations pursuant to Article 2 shall remain valid for the Recipient even after the end of this agreemen t for a period of one (1) years thereafter.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the expiration date of the agreement from a fixed date to an indefinite period under certain conditions. It also changes the validity of obligations pursuant to Article 2 from five years to one year. This creates a direct contradiction with the original fixed expiration date and the original five-year validity period, as noted in the provided explanation. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, but this does not negate the contradiction that now exists within the agreement.
